rationale: Fundamentally unprovable: postcondition requires r1.id == r2.id (spec equality on Vec<u8>), but Verus's spec equality on Vec is uninterpreted — it does NOT follow from view equality (r1.id@ == r2.id@). Verified this by minimal test: a proof fn with (v1@ == v2@) ==> v1 == v2 fails verification in this Verus version (0.2026.04.03). The premise r1@ == ep@ && r2@ == ep@ only gives r1.id@ == r2.id@ via AbstractEndPoint structural equality, which cannot lift to Vec spec ==. lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq relates PartialEq::eq_spec to view ==, not spec ==. Attempted: (1) chain r1==ep, r2==ep, r1.id==r2.id; (2) =~= extensional; (3) assert by(compute); (4) broadcast lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq. All fail. The clone_end_point spec only guarantees cloned_ep@ == ep@, so two clones can produce Vecs with same view but different identities — determinism on raw Vec equality is genuinely false.
